name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Root Certificate Installed via Certutil
notes:
This building block detects adversaries installing a root certificate on a compromised system to avoid warnings when connecting to adversary controlled web servers.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: 'oscd.community, @redcanary, Zach Stanford @svch0st'
SIGMA Title: Root Certificate Installed
SIGMA Description: Adversaries may install a root certificate on a compromised system to avoid warnings when connecting to adversary controlled web servers.


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Root Certificate Installed via Certutil on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") MATCHES 'certutil\.exe' AQL filter query
when the event matches Command (custom) contains all of [-addstore or root]
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Root Certificate Installed via Certutil on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") MATCHES 'certutil\.exe' AQL filter query
AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains all of [-addstore or root]
